tables.name: users
tables.comments: Application identity and role profile.

tables.name: doctors
tables.comments: Doctor/provider profile linked to a user.

tables.name: predoctors
tables.comments: Assistant role that prepares prechecks.

tables.name: patients
tables.comments: Clinical patient profile linked to a user.

tables.name: patient_insurance_policies
tables.comments: Patient insurance policy details.

tables.name: appointments
tables.comments: Booked visit between patient and doctor.

tables.name: clinics
tables.comments: Clinic location where visits happen.

tables.name: visit_types
tables.comments: Visit type, duration, and billing defaults.

tables.name: encounters
tables.comments: Clinical visit record opened from an appointment.

tables.name: clinical_note_drafts
tables.comments: Temporary draft before a clinical note is saved.

tables.name: clinical_notes
tables.comments: Doctor observations saved in the encounter record.

tables.name: diagnoses
tables.comments: Diagnosis entries captured during an encounter.

tables.name: prescriptions
tables.comments: Medication order created by the doctor.

tables.name: lab_orders
tables.comments: Lab request created from an encounter.

tables.name: imaging_orders
tables.comments: Imaging request created from an encounter.

tables.name: clinical_documents
tables.comments: Document metadata attached to an encounter.

tables.name: document_attachments
tables.comments: Files connected to a clinical document.

tables.name: precheck_forms
tables.comments: Predoctor precheck form for a visit.

tables.name: care_tasks
tables.comments: Follow-up task for clinical or operational work.

tables.name: conversations
tables.comments: Message thread between patient and clinic users.

tables.name: messages
tables.comments: Individual message content and sender.

tables.name: payments
tables.comments: Payment record linked to patient, doctor, or visit.

tables.name: insurance_claims
tables.comments: Insurance claim raised from a clinical visit.

notes.content: Read: FK > owner. Areas group schemas. Lines should flow left to right.
appointment opens encounter; encounter owns notes, orders, files, tasks, messages, and billing.
